coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of calling a hosted LLM through an inference API and reading its response
Elementary grasp of prompt formatting and system messages for a chat model
Able to integrate a single model call into an application endpoint with supervision
Basic understanding of retrieval-augmented generation as fetching context before a prompt
Able to load documents into a vector store following a provided example
Elementary notions of measuring response latency and token usage of a call
Basic understanding of structured output such as JSON responses from a model
complementaryCompetencies: Comfortable with Python and REST APIs
Familiar with environment variables and API key handling
Elementary notion of embeddings as numeric representations of text
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Can complete simple integration tasks with detailed guidance
Needs 3-6 months to become productive

coreCompetencies: Able to build a basic RAG pipeline that retrieves chunks and injects them into a prompt
Grasp of chunking strategies and embedding models for indexing documents
Able to integrate an inference API into a service with error handling and retries
Practical use of prompt templates and few-shot examples for a task
Able to enforce structured output with schema validation of model responses
Grasp of token limits and context-window budgeting for a request
Familiar with caching model responses to reduce cost and latency
complementaryCompetencies: Comfortable with vector databases such as pgvector or a managed index
Familiar with streaming responses to the client
Basic practice of unit testing prompts and integration code
indicators: Develops applied AI features with supervision
Resolves medium-complexity issues in inference integration
Actively participates in code and prompt reviews

coreCompetencies: Proficient at building production RAG with reranking and metadata filtering
Able to design offline evaluation sets that score answer relevance and grounding
Able to detect and mitigate hallucinations through citation and grounding checks
Solid command of prompt engineering including task decomposition and output constraints
Able to instrument model calls with tracing of prompts, tokens and latency
Grasp of model-selection trade-offs across quality, cost and speed
Able to implement fallback logic across providers when a model call fails
complementaryCompetencies: Aware of prompt-injection risks in retrieval contexts and their basic mitigations
Able to build hybrid search combining keyword and vector retrieval
Basic practice of function calling to connect models to application logic
indicators: Completes complex applied AI features with minimal supervision
Identifies and resolves failures in retrieval and inference pipelines
Actively contributes to the technical design of AI features

coreCompetencies: Able to architect end-to-end AI features integrating retrieval, inference and business logic
Proficient at evaluation-driven development with automated scoring of quality regressions
Able to design multi-step LLM workflows that chain retrieval, tools and generation
Solid command of optimizing cost and latency through caching, batching and model routing
Able to build online quality monitoring that flags drift in production model outputs
Proficient with retrieval architectures including chunk optimization and embedding refresh
Able to run A/B tests comparing prompts, models and retrieval strategies in production
complementaryCompetencies: Aware of fine-tuning and when it beats prompting for a task
Able to design guardrails that filter unsafe or off-topic model responses
Grasp of data privacy when sending user data to model providers
indicators: Leads development of critical AI product features
Effectively mentors junior engineers
Makes informed architectural decisions on AI integration

coreCompetencies: Expert at designing production-grade LLM application platforms serving live traffic
Able to architect scalable inference serving with load balancing across model providers
Proficient with evaluation pipelines combining offline benchmarks and online quality signals
Able to design retrieval systems that stay fresh and scale to large corpora
Solid command of controlling cost per request at scale through routing and quantized models
Able to build observability that captures prompts, retrieval, outputs and user feedback
Expert at mitigating hallucination and prompt injection in production systems
complementaryCompetencies: Aware of fine-tuning and adapter training for domain specialization
Able to design evaluation benchmarks for specific product tasks
Grasp of compliance concerns when processing user data through models
indicators: Designs AI architectures for complete products
Defines team technical standards for applied AI development
Leads cross-team initiatives on AI infrastructure

coreCompetencies: Able to architect enterprise AI platforms that serve model-powered features to many teams
Expert at governance for model usage including cost controls and access policies
Proficient with evaluation infrastructure and continuous quality monitoring across products
Able to design systems resilient to model-provider outages and version changes
Solid command of security threats specific to LLM applications such as data leakage and injection
Able to lead migrations that adopt AI capabilities across existing products at scale
Expert at reliability engineering for probabilistic model-driven features
complementaryCompetencies: Aware of self-hosted open-weight model deployment as an alternative to APIs
Able to design shared retrieval and evaluation infrastructure for the organization
Grasp of vendor negotiation and model-licensing economics
indicators: Architects AI solutions for multiple teams
Influences organizational technical decisions on applied AI
Represents the company technically in the applied AI domain

coreCompetencies: Provides technical leadership for enterprise-wide adoption of model-powered products
Able to design AI application architectures for large product portfolios
Expert at strategies for controlling quality and cost of AI features at organizational scale
Solid command of vendor-agnostic and model-agnostic application architectures
Able to define multi-year roadmaps for applied AI capabilities
Deep grasp of the total cost of ownership of LLM-based systems
Expert at building and scaling applied AI engineering teams
complementaryCompetencies: Able to evaluate emerging models and inference tooling for adoption
Able to represent applied AI engineering with technical partners
Aware of industry trends in inference optimization and retrieval
indicators: Defines organizational technical strategy for applied AI
Recognized as a technical authority in applied AI systems
Speaks at industry conferences

coreCompetencies: Sets the strategic vision for how model-powered products evolve within the organization
Able to lead major shifts in how the company builds and ships AI features
Expert at technical due diligence for AI-driven products and acquisitions
Drives organizational change toward AI-augmented product development
Able to build and lead applied AI engineering organizations
Deep grasp of the business impact of model quality, cost and reliability decisions
Expert at executive stakeholder relations around AI product risk
complementaryCompetencies: Able to influence industry practice on applied AI engineering
Able to guide investment decisions in AI tooling and infrastructure
